title: Firebase packages (Flutter)

sections.markdown: Firebase on Flutter (FlutterFire) is a family of packages: `firebase_core` (always required) plus one per product — `firebase_auth`, `cloud_firestore`, `firebase_messaging`, etc. `flutterfire_cli` generates `firebase_options.dart` per project.

Multi-env is the classic gotcha. Run `flutterfire configure` twice for dev/prod — write to different files (`firebase_options_dev.dart`) — then choose at runtime via `--dart-define` (flavor). This keeps analytics/crashlytics separated per environment.

Version mismatches across packages (firebase_core 2.x with firebase_auth 3.x from different times) cause native build failures. Upgrade FlutterFire packages together and check the compatibility matrix.
Enable App Check + turn on offline persistence where it matters (Firestore, RTDB). Firebase Emulator Suite lets you develop and test locally without touching production.

flashcards.back: firebase_core; every other Firebase package depends on it.
flutterfire_cli — generates firebase_options.dart per project + platforms.
Configure twice; choose the right firebase_options at runtime via flavor / dart-define.

quiz.q: Every Firebase Flutter app must add…
For local development without touching production, use…

challenges.text: Add firebase_core + one product; call Firebase.initializeApp.
Set up flavors (dev/prod) with separate firebase_options files.
Run Firestore + Auth against the local Emulator Suite in tests.
Enable App Check across iOS + Android + Web.

interviewQuestions: How does FlutterFire package structure work?
How do you handle multiple environments?
How do you test with the Emulator Suite?
